changes_made: Renamed k8s_rbac_service_account_unused_disabled to k8s_rbac_unused_service_accounts_disabled for clarity and consistency
Renamed k8s_rbac_role_binding_unused_disabled to k8s_rbac_unused_role_bindings_disabled for clarity and consistency
Renamed k8s_rbac_cluster_role_binding_unused_disabled to k8s_rbac_unused_cluster_role_bindings_disabled for clarity and consistency
review_notes: The control AC-2(3) is applicable to Kubernetes as it involves managing and disabling unused accounts, which can be mapped to RBAC resources in Kubernetes. The original function names were slightly awkward and inconsistent with typical naming conventions. Adjustments were made to improve clarity and consistency, ensuring they clearly describe the action of disabling unused RBAC resources.
naming_improvements: Ensure the order of words in function names reflects the action and resource clearly, e.g., 'unused_service_accounts_disabled' instead of 'service_account_unused_disabled'.
title: Account Management - Account management | disable accounts disable accounts within [ assignment
step1_reasoning: The control AC-2(3) focuses on disabling unused accounts, which can be mapped to Kubernetes by ensuring that unused service accounts, role bindings, and cluster role bindings are disabled or removed. This aligns with the principle of least privilege and reducing attack surface by ensuring that only necessary accounts and bindings are active.
